stem: A candidate enters the hiring pipeline. The probability of passing resume screening is 73%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing phone interview is 83%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing onsite interview is between 75% and 94% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the candidate is hired?

ground_truth.answer: P(hired) ranges from 0.454 to 0.57 as the onsite interview pass rate varies from 75% to 94%. Point estimate: 0.509.

solution_steps: The candidate must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the onsite interview stage creates a 11.6 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the onsite interview rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
